data.title: Webcast Alert: Cavco Industries, Inc. Announces Fiscal 2025 Fourth Quarter and Year End Earnings Release and Conference Call Webcast
data.description:
PHOENIX, May 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Cavco Industries, Inc. will release earnings for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ended March 29, 2025 on Thursday, May 22, 2025 after the close of market. Senior management will discuss the results in a live webcast the following day, Friday, May 23, 2025 at 10:00 a.m. Eastern Time.

Date:   May 23, 2025

Time:   10:00 a.m. ET

Listen via Internet: https://investor.cavco.com/

Listen via Telephone: To participate in the call, please register here to receive the dial-in number and your unique PIN.

If you are unable to participate during the live webcast, the call will be available for 60 days on https://investor.cavco.com/.

Cavco Industries, Inc., headquartered in Phoenix, Arizona, designs and produces factory-built housing products primarily distributed through a network of independent and Company-owned retailers. We are one of the largest producers of manufactured and modular homes in the United States, based on reported wholesale shipments. We are also a leading producer of park model RVs, vacation cabins and factory-built commercial structures. Cavco's finance subsidiary, CountryPlace Mortgage, is an approved Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac seller/servicer and a Ginnie Mae mortgage-backed securities issuer that offers conforming mortgages, non-conforming mortgages and home-only loans to purchasers of factory-built homes. Our insurance subsidiary, Standard Casualty, provides property and casualty insurance to owners of manufactured homes.

Mark Fusler

Corporate Controller and Investor Relations

Investor_relations@cavco.com

Phone: 602-256-6263

On the Internet: www.cavcoindustries.com

data.title: Philippines cash remittances up 2.6% y/y in March
data.description:
Cash remittances rose 2.6% from a year earlier to $2.81 billion in March, the Philippine central bank said on Thursday.

Cash remittances rose by 2.7% percent to $8.44 billion in January-March from S$8.22 billion in the same period a year earlier.

data.title: HK-listed Tencent climbs on Q1 revenue beat
data.description:
** Tencent Holdings climbs 2.1% to HK$532, highest since March 20

** World's largest video game company and WeChat operator's Q1 rev of 180 bln yuan ($24.94 bln), a 13% y/y rise, beats LSEG analysts' estimate of 174.6 bln yuan

** Jefferies maintains "buy", lifts PT to HK$665 from HK$646, citing co's Q1 results beat with solid performance across segments

** Citi keeps "buy", raises PT to HK$695 from HK$681, saying co began 2025 with a strong Q1, identifying prudent approach in operating evergreen gaming titles and securing steady ad spending from advertisers

** YTD, stock gains ~25%

($1 = 7.2170 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Toray Industries' Profit Surges 256% in Fiscal 2024
data.description:
Toray Industries profit attributable to owners of the parent surged 256% to 77.9 billion yen for fiscal 2024, up from 21.90 billion yen a year earlier.

The textile company's earnings per share jumped to 48.84 yen from 13.65 yen, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on May 14.

Revenue edged up 4% to 2.56 trillion yen in the year ended March 31 from 2.465 trillion yen.

The company declared a year-end dividend of 9.00 yen per share, payable from June 27.

For fiscal 2025, Toray forecasts profit attributable to owners of 82.00 billion yen or 52.44 yen per share, while revenue is expected to grow to 2.67 trillion yen.

It expects to pay out interim and final dividends of 10 yen per share, each, for the current year.

data.title: Sojitz Corp - To Acquire 66.5% Stake In Nippon A&L Inc
data.description:
Sojitz Corp :

  • TO ACQUIRE 66.5% STAKE IN NIPPON A&L INC

data.title: Australia employment surges past expectations in April, jobless rate steady
data.description:
Australian employment blew past expectations in April, driven by an increase in full-time roles for women, data showed on Thursday, while the unemployment rate held steady as an even larger number of people sought employment.

The better-than-expected headline figure sent the Australian dollar 0.3% higher to $0.6446, while three-year bond futures (YTTc1) extended earlier declines to be down 10 ticks at 96.29.

Still, investors remain confident the Reserve Bank of Australia will cut rates by a quarter point to 3.85% at its May 20 meeting, due to cooling inflation at home and global uncertainty caused by U.S. tariff policies. (0#AUDIRPR)

Figures from the Australian Bureau of Statistics showed net employment jumped 89,000 in April from March, when they rose an upwardly revised 36,400. That was far above market forecasts for a 20,000 rise and the biggest monthly increase in over a year.

The jobless rate held at 4.1%, as expected, as the participation rate climbed back to near record highs at 67.1%.

Despite the strength in employment, hours worked were unchanged, after two months of falls.

The RBA held rates steady at 4.1% in April but opened the door to a rate cut by stating that the meeting in May would be an opportunity to revisit monetary policy settings.

Global tariff risks and a disappointing recovery in consumer spending have clouded the economic outlook for Australia but the labour market has so far proven resilient.

The unemployment rate remains low and job advertisements are stabilising above pre-COVID levels. Wages have been well-behaved, with growth in the private sector mostly subdued.

The central bank is not expecting further loosening in the labour market, tipping unemployment to peak at 4.2% this cycle.

data.title: Thai Beverage's Outlook Appears Challenging — Market Talk
data.description: Thai Beverage's outlook appears challenging after its 1H FY 2025 results missed expectations, UOB Kay Hian analysts say in a research report. For 2H FY 2025, the brokerage has become more pessimistic on the beverage company's beer segment owing to recent macroeconomic uncertainty that has hurt sales volumes, specifically that of Vietnamese subsidiary Sabeco. The Singapore-listed company's overall sales volume is likely to only grow by a low single-digit percentage in 2H FY 2025, partly due to a weakening Thai economy, the analysts add. The brokerage downgrades the stock's rating to hold from buy and lowers the target price to S$0.51 from S$0.56. Shares are 1.0% lower at S$0.475. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Delta Lithium to Spin Off Mt Ida Gold Asset into New IPO-Bound Explorer Ballard Mining, Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Delta Lithium said it is demerging its Mt Ida Gold asset into a new, standalone, gold-focused Australia-listed exploration company called Ballard Mining as it intends to separate its gold and lithium assets, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

As part of the concurrent initial public offering, Ballard intends to raise between AU$25 million and AU$30 million, before costs, while eligible Delta shareholders will also be entitled to participate in a priority offer.

Former De Grey Chairman Simon Lill will be appointed as non-executive chairman of the new company, and Tim Manners will be its finance director.

The company said eligible Delta shareholders are expected to receive one Ballard Share for every 11.25 fully paid ordinary shares in Delta through an in-specie distribution.

The company will retain a substantial stake in Ballard to keep an "upside" exposure, the filing added.

The company's shares rose nearly 3% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Chinese Banks See Drop in New Loans in April
data.description:
Banks in China extended 280 billion yuan in new loans in April, according to calculations by MT Newswires based on data from the People's Bank of China released Wednesday.

This was lower than the 700 billion yuan in new yuan-denominated loans expected by analysts polled by Reuters and a drop from the 3.64 trillion yuan reported in March.

Household loans increased by 518.4 billion yuan while loans to enterprises rose by 9.27 trillion yuan.

data.title: Airports of Thailand Target Price Lowered to THB30.00 From THB45.00 by Thanachart Securities >AOT.TH
Airports of Thailand Cut to Sell From Buy by Thanachart Securities >AOT.TH
Dems seek suspicious activity reports linked to Trump crypto ventures
data.description:
US Democrat lawmakers have sent a letter to the US Treasury demanding access to suspicious activity reports (SARs) on several Trump-backed crypto projects as part of the latest probe into the president’s digital ventures. 

Penned by representatives Gerald Connolly, Joseph Morelle, and Jamie Raskin, the May 14 letter asks Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent for all SARS filed since 2023 related to World Liberty Financial (WLF) and the Official Trump (TRUMP) token. 

Financial institutions in the US must file SARs with the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network, a bureau within the Department of the Treasury, when they detect suspicious activity, including potential money laundering or fraud. 

The sweeping probe asks for any SARs mentioning WinRed, America PAC, Elon Musk, political action committee, PAC, Trump, World Liberty Financial, WLF, TRUMP, MELANIA and Justin Sun, no later than May 30. 

The Democratic lawmakers say their probe is to “determine whether legislation is necessary to prevent violations of campaign finance, consumer protection, bribery, securities fraud, and other anti-corruption laws” and to guard against “financial misconduct connected to prospective or current federal officials.” 

Democrats argue WLF and Trump coin could be misused

As part of the letter, the lawmakers argue WLF could be misused as a “vehicle for foreign influence peddling” because it served part of its token sale for foreign investors, who are “generally subject to less stringent regulation than US investors.” 

Justin Sun’s investment in WLF and the subsequent pause of the SEC’s lawsuit that alleged the crypto entrepreneur broke securities laws has also been flagged as a concern. 

Trump’s token has come under fire as well because the lawmakers argue in their letter that the identities of the coin purchasers are not publicly disclosed, which could open the door for bad actors to “curry favor with Trump” by purchasing the coin. 

At the same time, SARS related to Republican digital fundraising WinRed, Elon Musk’s super PAC, which poured $250 million into Trump’s election campaign, and two other PACs are being sought. 

This effort is the latest Democrat-led salvo against Trump’s crypto ventures.

A group of Democratic senators reportedly sent a letter to leadership at the US Department of Justice and the Treasury Department expressing concerns about Trump’s ties to crypto exchange Binance and potential conflicts of interest in regulating the industry, according to a May 9 Bloomberg report. 

US Democratic lawmakers also launched a multi-angle attack on May 6, targeting Trump’s ability to profit from his crypto initiatives with two bills and a subcommittee inquiry.

data.title: Savers Value Village Isn't Selling Any Shares in Offering, Won't Receive Any Proceeds From Sale of Shares by Selling Holders >SVV
China Rolls Back Rare Earth Export Ban in Trade War Truce with U.S.
data.description:
China lifted export restrictions on rare earths and military-use technologies for 28 U.S. entities just two days after a major U.S.-China deal to temporarily lower tariffs.

The Geneva Agreement, reached on May 12, includes a 90-day pause and a 115-percentage-point reduction in reciprocal tariffs, signaling the first major de-escalation in the ongoing trade war.

China's Ministry of Commerce confirmed the suspension, effective Wednesday, allowing exporters to apply for licenses to resume shipments during the 90-day window.

This follows a period of heightened tensions, during which the U.S. imposed tariffs of up to 245% and China retaliated with rare earth export bans affecting 28 U.S. firms. These materials—vital for chips, EVs, and military tech—include samarium, gadolinium, and dysprosium.

In a further sign of thawing relations, China also announced a temporary lift on trade and investment bans for 17 U.S. companies, calling the latest developments a potential reset in bilateral ties.

data.title: Centuria Office REIT Optimistic on Australian Office Markets Over Medium-Term
data.description:
Centuria Office REIT is optimistic on Australian office markets over the medium-term, underpinned by diminishing forecast supply, elevated replacement costs associated with development and capital expenditure requirements, and signs of improving net absorption and reduced sub-leasing activity, its fund manager, Belinda Cheung said in a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

Centuria Office REIT said that 8,467 square meters of lease terms were agreed across 11 separate transactions in the March quarter, including 7,011 square meters of new leases.

It reported a portfolio occupancy of 91.4%.

The REIT reaffirmed its fiscal year funds from operation guidance of AU$0.118 per unit and distribution guidance of AU$0.101 per unit.

data.title: Navigator Holdings Ltd reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Navigator Holdings Ltd reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 39 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 31 cents. The mean expectation of six analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 31 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 29 cents to 38 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 12.9% to $151.41 million from a year ago; analysts expected $146.30 million.

  • Navigator Holdings Ltd's reported EPS for the quarter was 39 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $27.04 million.

  • Navigator Holdings Ltd shares had risen by 4.3% this quarter and lost 9.6% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 8.5% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "strong buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 8 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Navigator Holdings Ltd is $22.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data May 15 at 02:14 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Surging Australian Jobs Growth in April Could Trouble RBA
data.description:
By James Glynn

SYDNEY--Australian employment growth in April surged by more than four times what economists expected, which could cast doubt on a widely anticipated rate cut by the Reserve Bank of Australia next week.

Employment jumped by 89,000 in April compared with an expected increase of just 21,000, while the unemployment rate was unchanged at 4.1%, the Australian Bureau of Statistics said Thursday.

The big jump in employment follows news on Wednesday of renewed strength in wages growth in the first quarter, and counters the idea that the Australian economy is weak against the backdrop of a slowing global growth outlook.

Employment grew by 390,000, or 2.7%, over the year to April, with the rate of increase higher than population growth, the ABS said.

The jump in employment in April was biased toward full-time work, which grew by 59,500 over the month, the data showed.

The workforce participation rate also jumped to 67.1% in April from 66.8% in March.

The strong employment and wages data will likely spark warnings about Australia's weak productivity growth performance over the last decade, and the potential for inflation to linger in a tight job market.

Economists said the RBA could still cut interest rates next week, citing the fact that both headline and core measures of inflation are back within the 2% to 3% target band.

Still, the strength in employment growth suggest the depth of cuts further out in the year will be shallower.

The underemployment rate rose by 0.1 percentage point to 6.0% in April, 0.5 percentage point lower than a year earlier, the ABS said.

Write to James Glynn at james.glynn@wsj.com

data.title: Savers Value Village Expects Offering to Close May 16 >SVV
Germany, Philippines sign defence agreement
data.description:
Germany and the Philippines have agreed to enhance defence ties and boost joint activities as Manila builds up a range of alliances to strengthen its position in a longstanding dispute with China in the contested South China Sea.

Philippine defence secretary Gilberto Teodoro and German counterpart Boris Pistorius signed an "arrangement concerning defence cooperation" in Berlin on Wednesday, agreeing to expand cooperation to include cyber security, defence armament and logistics and United Nations peacekeeping, Manila's defence ministry announced on Thursday.

The deal follows a visit by Pistorius to Manila last year where he and Teodoro committed to boosting long-term relations between their militaries.

The Philippines' defence cooperation arrangement with Berlin adds to a growing list of defence deals beyond its traditional ally the United States.

Last month, the Philippines signed a defence agreement with New Zealand for expanded military cooperation, and a similar deal with Canada is expected to be signed soon.

A reciprocal access deal with Japan was ratified in December, and President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has approved the start of negotiations with France for a visiting forces agreement.

Beijing claims almost the entire South China Sea, a vital trade artery, despite overlapping maritime claims by Brunei, Malaysia, the Philippines and Vietnam, angering its neighbours.

In 2016, the Permanent Court of Arbitration in the Hague said Beijing's claims had no legal basis. China rejects that decision.

Pistorius said in Manila last August that the "ruling remains valid, without any exceptions".

In September last year, two German warships went on a rare transit in the Indo-Pacific to demonstrate Berlin's commitment to freedom of navigation.

data.title: Shenzhen UUGreenPower Files for Shenzhen IPO
data.description:
Shenzhen UUGreenPower (SHE:301590) filed for an initial public offering of 10.5 million shares on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange's ChiNext board.

IPO proceeds will go towards building a charging module production base, the headquarters, and research and development center as well as for supplementary working capital, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The expected date of issuance will be May 26.

Minsheng Securities serves as lead underwriter.

Shenzhen UUGreenPower produces core components of new energy vehicle charging equipment.

data.title: Tencent's AI Investment Should Position It for Future Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Tencent's AI investment should position it well for future growth, Barclays analysts say in a research note. While AI has tangibly benefited Tencent's ads and games sectors, it's a discovery process for both the company and users in other areas, the analysts say. In response to the recent Nvidia H20 export restriction, management noted during the earnings call that it's capable of pursing its AI strategies using previously acquired chips. The analysts think that "management recognized the fluid situation with regards to chip procurement [and] therefore has been allocating these chips to areas where immediate tangible returns can be achieved." Management believes that as its computing requirements gradually move to inferencing, they can use non-Nvidia chips to fulfill the growing needs. Shares are up 0.2% at HK$522.00. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Toyota clan needs to shore up its influence
data.description:
Toyota Motor’s founding family no longer has a tight grip on the wheel. Akio Toyoda, whose grandfather established the world’s largest carmaker, owns less than 1% of the $250 billion marque he chairs. Now a corporate overhaul in Japan is weakening the web of cross-shareholdings he relies on to wield influence. A buyout of a related company, Toyota Industries , would help the clan to shore up its position.

So-called "cross-shareholdings" are rife in Japan. These are held by companies to maintain and strengthen relationships with other businesses, and offer a support base for Toyoda. Toyota group companies plus partners like Suzuki Motor and Mazda Motor account for around 17% of Toyota Motor’s outstanding shares, excluding treasury stock, LSEG data shows. Suppliers and others such as Kobe Steel and KDDI , in which Toyota has invested, also hold stakes.

But these setups are falling out of favour: Japan’s Financial Services Agency is demanding more disclosure on these holdings; insurers and companies are under pressure to trim such positions too. Toyota Motor has reduced the number of these holdings to 126 as of September, from 228 in 2020. It complements a shareholder value push championed by bourse operator Japan Exchange Group.

As these arrangements unravel, Toyoda’s influence is diminishing. He's feeling shareholder heat, too. Votes in favour of his re-election fell to 72% at last year’s annual general meeting: without friendly backers, his rating could have slid closer to the 50% threshold required to keep the role. Only 34% of overseas institutional investors voted in his favour, as Toyoda himself noted at the time.

While some powerful Asian industrialists do own small stakes in the crown jewels of their empires, Toyoda's miniscule direct stake in the carmaker is even lower than the 1.63% stake Jay Y. Lee leverages to hold sway over South Korea's $266 billion Samsung Electronics .

Enter the 6 trillion yen ($42 billion) plan, first reported by Bloomberg, for Toyoda to buy out Toyota Industries. Toyota Motors may also back a deal: it's exploring a partial investment in Toyota Industries, which owns 9% of the carmaker.

That shareholding, worth 3.4 trillion yen, about $24 billion, offers a key to resolving Toyoda's problems. It equates to about 65% of Toyota Industries’ market capitalisation. Add in stakes it owns in 27 other companies, and there’s precious little value ascribed to its own operations.

If Toyoda wants to reinforce his influence with greater ownership, the buyout for Toyota Industries could turn into a real battle.

Follow @KatrinaHamlin on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Toyota Industries is considering “all possibilities” to enhance shareholder value and has received “various proposals” such as going private through a special purpose company, the company said in a filing on April 26.

Toyota Motor is considering investing in a privatisation of Toyota Industries, the carmaker said in a separate filing on April 26.

A day earlier, Bloomberg reported that Toyota Motor Chair Akio Toyoda had proposed a buyout of Toyota Industries that would value the company at 6 trillion yen ($42 billion), citing unnamed sources.

data.title: Brigade Enterprises Acquires Land in Chennai, India for New Residential Project
data.description:
Real estate developer Brigade Enterprises has acquired a 5.4-acre land parcel in Chennai, India, for 4.42 billion Indian rupees to develop a premium residential development, according to a Wednesday filing to the indian bourses.

The proposed project has a total development potential of 0.8 million square feet and a gross development value of around 16 billion rupees.

data.title: Australia PM Albanese to meet Indonesia counterpart in first international visit since re-election
data.description:
Key points:
  • PM Albanese met with President Prabowo informally after arriving on Wednesday
  • Albanese prioritises defence and economic ties with Indonesia
  • Australia-Indonesia relationship stable despite differing views on global powers

Australia's Prime Minister Anthony Albanese will meet with Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto on Thursday to discuss defence cooperation and global trade, after arriving in Jakarta on his first international visit since his re-election.

Albanese, sworn into office on Tuesday after his centre-left Labor party won an increased majority in parliament, said his visit showed the priority Canberra placed on defence and economic ties with Jakarta.

Albanese said the visit to one of the world's largest democracies was "an important step forward in the relationship".

"We're talking about further strengthening our defence ties and also how we put our economic partnership, how we can build on that with investment," he told reporters in Jakarta on Thursday.

An informal meeting with Prabowo on Wednesday evening ahead of official talks showed the depth of the relationship, he said.

Australia struck a defence cooperation treaty with Indonesia last year, and Australia was hosting defence and maritime security training for Indonesian forces, Albanese said.

Indonesia dismissed reports last month that Russia had requested to base military aircraft in the archipelago's easternmost province of Papua, about 1,200 km (750 miles) north of the Australian city of Darwin, where a U.S. Marine Corps rotational force is based for six months of the year.

"Indonesia's answer is no, they've made it very clear," Albanese told reporters on Thursday when asked about the matter.

Russia will try to increase its influence in the region and Australia is responding by building ties with neighbours including Indonesia, he added.

Australian Strategic Policy Institute senior analyst for defence, Euan Graham, said the Australia-Indonesia relationship has "avoided serious crisis for more than a decade, cooperation continues to move forward incrementally and there is greater stability than before".

Yet wide differences remain, he added.

"Jakarta sees China and Russia as vectors of opportunity more than threats and views the U.S. and China primarily through the same lens of great power rivalry. That's largely at odds with Canberra's world view," he said.

Albanese said the two leaders will also discuss global trade.

Indonesia is projected to be the fifth largest economy in the world by the end of the next decade.

Australia wants to increase economic ties with Southeast Asia, as it seeks to diversify export markets to reduce reliance on China, and in response to trade uncertainty caused by U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs.

Indonesia remains a "protected and challenging market" and a competitor to Australia in commodity exports, said Graham.

data.title: Dollar Struggles Amid Trade Policy Uncertainty
data.description:
The US dollar index edged lower to around 100.9 on Thursday, remaining under pressure as investors weighed persistent trade-related uncertainties despite a recent easing in tensions.

The greenback weakened notably against several Asian currencies, particularly the South Korean won, amid speculation that Washington is advocating for a weaker dollar as part of ongoing trade negotiations.

The Trump administration has argued that the relative strength of the dollar—and the weakness of regional currencies—has disadvantaged US exporters.

The dollar's recent rally, fueled by optimism over US-China tariff reductions, has also begun to fade as market focus shifts back to the broader economic implications of US trade policy.

Looking ahead, investors are eyeing US retail sales and producer inflation data due Thursday for fresh insight into consumer demand and inflation trends.

data.title: Tokyo Tatemono Profit Rises 23% in Q1
data.description:
Tokyo Tatemono's profit attributable to owners of the parent rose 23% to 14.4 billion yen in the first quarter of 2025, from 11.7 billion a year earlier.

The real estate company's basic earnings per share increased to 68.77 yen from 55.82 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on May 9.

Operating revenue grew 9.0% to 126.7 billion yen, up from 116.2 billion yen in the same period last year.

For the full year ending Dec. 31, Tokyo Tatemono forecasts attributable profit of 55.00 billion yen or 264.44 yen per share, while operating revenue is expected to rise to 503.00 billion yen.

The company expects to pay an interim and final dividend of 48 yen and 49 yen per share, respectively, for the current year.

data.title: Mitsubishi Electric Building Solutions Launches a New Projection-type Hall Lantern for Overseas Markets
data.description:
TOKYO, May 15, 2025 - (JCN Newswire) - Mitsubishi Electric Building Solutions Corporation (MEBS, Head Office: Chiyoda-Ku, Tokyo; President: Iwao Oda) today announced that it has developed of a new projection-type lantern for use in elevator halls for overseas markets, and that this product will be launched on May 20, 2025.

Combining industry-first innovation with a simple, minimalist design that emphasizes functionality while blending in with the existing architecture, the new hall lantern will meet a variety of customer needs and contribute to increasing the value of buildings.

Main Features

1) Featuring a new projection method that realizes more flexible use of architectural space

- The new hall lantern adopts a new method of projecting light symbols from a compact body to indicate the direction of elevator travel. This cutting-edge idea enables greater flexibility in the use of architectural space.

2) Designed to Reduce the Construction Burden and Contribute to Sustainability

- Compared to conventional wall-embedded hall lanterns, the amount of wall space taken up by the new wall lantern is minimized and the impact on the wall is reduced. In addition, the new hall lantern is 90% lighter than a conventional product, which contributes to sustainability through improved manufacturing and transportation efficiency*2.

3) Contributing to increasing the value of buildings with design variations that meet diverse needs

- Projector case covers are available in a wide variety of colors, materials, and finishes. Projection signs can also be customized to meet customer needs*3 and harmonize with various architectural styles and interiors.

Development Background

Elevators, which play an important role in the architecture of buildings, are increasingly required to incorporate innovative designs and functions that blend in with the architectural space, in addition to facilitating ease of installation and minimizing their environmental impact. In overseas elevator markets as well, there are diverse needs in terms of flexible optimization of specifications to match the architecture, new user experiences, and reduced installation work, while keeping costs down.

To help meet these needs, MEBS has developed a new projection-type hall lantern. As the industry's first elevator direction display that makes use of projection, it delivers a novel and advanced user experience. Moreover, its compact case cover and customizable design ensure compatibility with a wide variety of architectural styles.

In addition to being advanced and sophisticated, the lightweight and compact design also saves on installation work. Compared to conventional wall-embedded hall lanterns, it reduces the impact on the building wall by minimizing the need to drill holes, while also shortening the installation time. What's more, it contributes to a reduced environmental impact by improving manufacturing and transportation efficiency.

Product Details

Sales Areas: All overseas regions

Applicable Models: All elevator models manufactured by MEBS

Size: Width 90 mm / Depth 85 mm / Height 40 mm

Price: By quote

*1 *2 According to MEBS's own research, as of April 2025

*3 Customization of the casing cover type and signature characters is available as an option or through special orders.

About Mitsubishi Electric Building Solutions Corporation

Mitsubishi Electric Building Solutions Corporation is a consolidated subsidiary of Mitsubishi Electric Corporation established in April 2022 that conducts a comprehensive range of operations in the building systems business, from development and manufacturing to maintenance and renewal. As a building solutions provider, we support the economic and social infrastructure through one-stop integrated solutions that combine a wide range of building-related products and services, including elevators, escalators, air conditioning and refrigeration equipment, and building systems, as well as with our extensive experience in building operation and management, and advanced digital technology. From buildings to building complexes and even entire cities, we contribute to enriching human life in buildings and urban spaces by solving a wide variety of issues that are closely linked to people and society, with the ultimate aim of realizing smart cities. For more information, please visit www.mebs.com/

data.title: Peet Appoints Interim CEO; Shares Down 5%
data.description:
Peet named Chief Financial Officer Brett Fullarton interim chief executive to temporarily succeed Brendan Gore, who will step down July 1 to pursue other interests, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Shares fell nearly 5% in morning trade Thursday.
